questions.question: Why does DDP broadcast parameters at construction instead of relying on the same seed across ranks?
options: Same-seed init drifts by float epsilon across ranks; broadcast guarantees byte-equal weights before training starts
questions.explanation: Float ops on different hardware (or even different process orders) can differ at the bit level. A broadcast from rank 0 is the only way to get byte-equal weights everywhere.

questions.question: What does DDP do after backward and before optimizer.step?
options: Allreduce-SUM every parameter's gradient and divide by world_size to get the mean gradient
questions.explanation: The mean gradient is invariant to world_size so a learning rate tuned at one rank works at N ranks.

questions.question: Why divide by world_size after allreduce-SUM instead of using allreduce-MEAN?
options: Allreduce-MEAN does not exist in gloo
Division by world_size on each rank is a local op; allreduce-MEAN would be implemented the same way under the hood
questions.explanation: Both approaches yield the same arithmetic. NCCL ships allreduce-AVG since 2.10; gloo does not, so divide locally after SUM.

questions.question: What does gradient bucketing in production DDP achieve?
options: Coalesces many small allreduces into one large allreduce, amortising the per-call latency floor
questions.explanation: A 1B-parameter model has thousands of grad tensors; one allreduce per tensor pays gloo or NCCL latency thousands of times.

questions.question: Why does no_sync() exist in PyTorch DDP?
options: Gradient accumulation over K microbatches needs to skip the post-backward allreduce for K-1 of them; no_sync wraps that pause
questions.explanation: Without no_sync the allreduce fires K times per accumulated step and throughput drops to the floor.

questions.question: Why does this lesson's reference implementation walk every rank's micro-batch sequentially in one process?
options: It gives a byte-equal baseline: the sequential mean gradient must equal DDP's allreduced mean gradient at every step
It is the only way to use gloo
questions.explanation: Equivalence to the reference loop is the load-bearing correctness test; without it gradient sync bugs hide until step 10000.
